rules.name: Blessings of the Lady
rules.description: Prior to battle the Bretonnia army may kneel and pray. If it does so the opposing player counts as winning the roll-off, but all models with this rule gain: a 6+ Ward save against any wounds suffered; a 5+ Ward save against wounds from attacks with Strength 5 or higher. Lost immediately if the model flees or (for characters) refuses a challenge. Falling Back in Good Order does not count as fleeing.

rules.name: Finest Warhorses
rules.description: When a unit with this rule makes a Charge, Flee or Pursuit roll, it may re-roll any dice that roll a natural 1, before discarding any dice required to be discarded.

rules.name: Lance Formation
rules.description: A unit with this rule may adopt a Lance formation. A Lance is a triangular formation: one model in the front rank, two in the second, three in the third, and so on. The unit may not turn or redress ranks, but may wheel, move backwards, move sideways and reform normally. In combat only the model at the front makes base contact. Rank Bonus +1 per rank. Combat Result +1. Blunted if charged in the flank.

rules.name: Peasantry
rules.description: If a unit with this rule is within 6" of a friendly model that has the Knight's Vow, the Questing Vow or the Grail Vow, and that model is not fleeing, the unit may use that model's Leadership characteristic instead of its own. A standard carried by a unit with this rule cannot be counted as a trophy of war. A character with this rule can only join a unit that also has this rule.

rules.name: Levies
rules.description: Peasant levy rule. Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook for full details.

rules.name: Peasant's Duty
rules.description: This character and any unit they have joined may choose to Give Ground rather than Fall Back in Good Order, and does not have to make a Panic test when a friendly unit of Levies Breaks and flees from combat whilst within 6" of it. Additionally, unless this character is fleeing, any friendly unit that is within their Command range and has the Levies special rule may re-roll a failed Panic test.

rules.name: Rallying Cry
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.name: The Knight's Vow
rules.description: A model with this Chivalrous Vow does not have to make a Panic test when a friendly unit with the Peasantry rule is destroyed whilst within 6", or when it is fled through by a friendly unit with the Peasantry rule. A unit with this Vow cannot be joined by a character with the Peasantry rule. A character with this Vow cannot join a unit with the Peasantry rule.

rules.name: The Questing Vow
rules.description: A model with this Chivalrous Vow has the Stubborn rule and can re-roll any failed Fear, Panic or Terror test. It does not have to make a Panic test from Peasantry units being destroyed or fled through within 6". It cannot be equipped with a lance. A unit with this Vow cannot be joined by a character with the Knight's Vow or Peasantry rule. A character with this Vow cannot join a unit with the Peasantry rule.

rules.name: The Grail Vow
rules.description: A model with this Chivalrous Vow has the Immune to Psychology, Magical Attacks and Stubborn rules. Models with this Vow always benefit from the Blessings of the Lady without praying at the start of the game. A model with this Vow cannot refuse a challenge. A unit with this Vow can only be joined by a character with the Grail Vow or a Handmaiden of the Lady. A character with this Vow cannot join a unit with the Peasantry rule.

rules.name: Aura of the Lady
rules.description: Any unit this character has joined gains the Magical Attacks special rule.

rules.name: Shield of the Lady
rules.description: If this character has joined a unit with Unit Strength 10 or more that has a Chivalrous Vow, they may voluntarily retire to the rear of the unit. If they do so, they can no longer attack or be attacked, but continue to confer Leadership and special rule benefits, and may cast spells as if within the fighting rank.

rules.name: Lore of the Lady
rules.description: A Wizard with this rule may discard one randomly generated spell and instead select the signature spell of their chosen Lore of Magic, or one of the Lore of the Lady spells (The Lady's Gift or The Lady's Wrath).

rules.name: Living Saints
rules.description: Every model in a unit of Grail Knights can issue and accept challenges in the same manner as a character.

rules.name: Grail Reliquae
rules.description: The Grail Reliquae is placed in the centre of the front rank. It counts as both a standard bearer and a musician. Casualties are removed normally but the Reliquae cannot lose Wounds while any Battle Pilgrims remain. Once all Battle Pilgrims are gone the Reliquae itself can lose Wounds. Whilst within 12" of a friendly model with the Grail Vow that is not fleeing, the unit gains the Immune to Psychology and Unbreakable rules.

rules.name: Retinue of the Saints
rules.description: Your army may include up to one Grail Reliquae for every character or unit with the Grail Vow it includes.

rules.name: Dispersed Formation
rules.description: Whilst in Skirmish formation, every model in a unit of Pegasus Knights must be within 2" of another model belonging to the same unit, rather than the usual 1".

rules.name: Beguiling Aura
rules.description: Enemy models must make a Leadership test before making any rolls To Hit against this model during the Combat phase. If this test is failed, only rolls of a natural 6 will hit.

rules.name: Immovable Object
rules.description: Once a field trebuchet has been placed on the battlefield during deployment, it cannot be moved by its crew during the Remaining Moves sub-phase. It may still pivot freely and make a follow-up move.

rules.name: Aura of the Fay
rules.description: When the Green Knight loses his last Wound, he is removed but not slain. During any Start of Turn sub-phase in which he is not on the battlefield, the controlling player may attempt to awaken him by rolling a D6: on 1-2 he slumbers until next turn, on 3+ he awakens. He awakens automatically by round five. Each time he is slain he suffers -1 to awakening rolls and -1 to his Wounds characteristic (minimum 1). The enemy can only win Victory Points for the Green Knight if he is not on the battlefield at game end.

rules.name: Blessed Knight
rules.description: The Green Knight has a 5+ Ward save against any wounds suffered.

rules.name: Guardian of the Sacred Sites
rules.description: The Green Knight is not placed during deployment. During Start of Turn sub-phases in which he is not on the battlefield, his controlling player may attempt to awaken him (see Aura of the Fay). When he awakens, he may be placed completely within any natural terrain feature on the battlefield. During Remaining Moves, if he is completely within a natural terrain feature and not in combat, he may teleport to any other natural terrain feature on the battlefield instead of moving normally.

rules.name: The Wyrm Slayer
rules.description: Should Sir Cecil Gastonne kill an enemy model whose troop type is monstrous infantry, monstrous cavalry, monstrous creature or behemoth during any Combat phase, he gains the Terror special rule for the remainder of the game.

rules.name: The Exile's Vow
rules.description: Sir Cecil Gastonne's personal Chivalrous Vow. Refer to the Arcane Journal: Kingdom of Bretonnia for full details.

rules.name: Arcane Backlash
rules.description: Lady Élisse may apply a +1 modifier to any of her Dispel rolls. In addition, should she roll any natural double when making a Dispel roll (not including a natural double 1), the spell is unbound and the casting Wizard immediately loses a single Wound.

rules.name: Untutored Arcanist
rules.description: When required to roll on the Miscast table, a Wizard with this rule must roll an extra D6 and discard the highest result.

rules.name: Crusading Knights
rules.description: In an Errantry Crusade army, the following characters and units gain the Crusader's Zeal special rule. Any of these characters or units that have the Knight's Vow may replace it with the Crusader's Vow for free.

rules.name: Crusader's Zeal
rules.description: A unit with this rule increases its maximum possible charge range by 1" and, when it makes a Charge roll, may apply a +1 modifier to the result. The unit also gains the Impetuous special rule.

rules.name: The Crusader's Vow
rules.description: A Chivalrous Vow. A model with this Vow has the Veteran special rule and does not have to make a Panic test when a friendly unit with the Peasantry rule is destroyed within 6" or fled through. A unit with this Vow cannot be joined by a character with the Peasantry rule. A character with this Vow cannot join a unit with the Peasantry rule.

rules.name: Earn Your Spurs
rules.description: Any enemy standard captured by a unit of Knights Errant is worth 100 Victory Points as a trophy of war. In addition, whilst within 6" of a friendly model with the Grail Vow or any Lord of Bretonnia, a unit of Knights Errant may re-roll any rolls To Hit of a natural 1.

rules.name: Magic Resistance (-1)
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.name: Magic Resistance (-2)
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.name: Armour Bane (2)
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.name: Armour Bane (3) — Impact Hits only
rules.description: This model's Impact Hits have the Armour Bane (3) special rule. Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook for the Armour Bane special rule.

rules.name: Armoured Hide (1)
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.name: Armoured Hide (1) — Chompa
rules.description: Chompa has Armoured Hide (1). Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook for the Armoured Hide special rule.

rules.name: Magical Attacks
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.name: Immune to Psychology
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.name: Hatred (All Enemies)

rules.name: Multiple Wounds (2)
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.name: Multiple Shots (D3)

rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.name: Random Attacks
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.name: Strike First
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.name: Impact Hits (1)
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.name: Impact Hits (D3)
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.name: Impact Hits (D6)

rules.name: Impact Hits (D3+1)
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.name: Multiple Wounds (D6)
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.name: Howdah
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.name: Regeneration (5+)
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.name: Mercenaries
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.name: Big 'Uns
rules.description: When engaged in combat, a model with this special rule has a +1 modifier to its Strength characteristic, and gains the Armour Bane (1) special rule.

rules.name: Choppas
rules.description: During a turn in which it charged, a model with this special rule may re-roll any rolls To Wound of a natural 1, and improves the Armour Piercing characteristic of its weapon(s) by 1. This special rule only applies to non-magical weapons and does not apply to a model's mount. If the model is using a magic weapon, this special rule ceases to apply.

rules.name: Da Boyz
rules.description: Your army must include one Black Orc Boss for every Black Orc Mob it includes, and vice versa. For each Black Orc Mob your army includes, it must also include one Black Orc War Boss or Big Boss. For each Black Orc War Boss or Big Boss your army includes, it must also include one Black Orc Mob.

rules.name: Fear of Elves
rules.description: Elves of any type cause Fear in models with this special rule.

rules.name: Ignore Goblin Panic
rules.description: This unit does not have to make a Panic test when a friendly Goblin unit is destroyed or Breaks and flees from combat whilst within 6" of it. Nor does this unit have to make a Panic test when it is fled through by a friendly Goblin unit. A Goblin unit is considered to be any unit made up entirely of Goblins of any kind, mounted or otherwise, including any war machine or chariot crewed entirely by Goblins. Should a Goblin unit be joined by an Orc character, it is no longer considered to be a Goblin unit.

rules.name: Ignore Panic
rules.description: This unit does not have to make a Panic test when a friendly unit that does not also have this special rule is destroyed or Breaks and flees from combat whilst within 6" of it. Nor does this unit have to make a Panic test when it is fled through by a friendly unit that does not also have this special rule.

rules.name: Quell Impetuosity
rules.description: Whilst within 6" of a unit with this special rule, friendly units may choose to ignore the Impetuous special rule.

rules.name: Waaagh!
rules.description: Once per game, during the Command sub-phase of their turn, this character may attempt to invoke the power of the Waaagh! by making a Leadership test (using their own Leadership). If this test is passed, until your next Start of Turn sub-phase this character, their mount and any Orc unit they have joined may re-roll any rolls To Hit of a natural 1 and, when calculating its combat result, may claim an additional bonus of +1 combat result point. An Orc unit is any unit made up entirely of Orcs or Black Orcs, mounted or otherwise. Should an Orc unit be joined by a Goblin character, it is no longer considered to be an Orc unit. This special rule is not cumulative.

rules.name: Mob Rule
rules.description: This Wizard applies a +1 modifier to their Casting rolls when they have joined an Orc unit (Orcs or Black Orcs) with Unit Strength 10 or more.

rules.name: Lore of Gork
rules.description: A Wizard with this rule may discard one randomly generated spell and instead select the signature spell of their chosen Lore of Magic, or one of the Lore of Gork spells (Brain Bursta or Gaze of Gork).

rules.name: Lore of Mork
rules.description: A Wizard with this rule may discard one randomly generated spell and instead select the signature spell of their chosen Lore of Magic, or one of the Lore of Mork spells (Mork's Curse or Itchy Nuisance).

rules.name: Hatred (Dwarfs)
rules.description: Models with this special rule have the Hatred special rule, but only against models with the Dwarf keyword. Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook for the Hatred special rule.

rules.name: Tusker Charge
rules.description: During a turn in which it charged, a War Boar's Tusks (hand weapon) have a Strength characteristic of S+1 and an Armour Piercing characteristic of -1. This special rule only applies to attacks made by a War Boar, not to their rider, a chariot or its crew.

rules.name: Chariot Runners
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook for the Chariot Runners special rule.

rules.name: Warpaint
rules.description: Warpaint gives its wearer a 6+ Ward save against any wounds suffered. However, a model with this special rule can never wear armour of any sort (though they may carry a shield).

rules.name: Skulking Menace
rules.description: Nasty Skulkers are not placed on the battlefield at the start of the game. Instead, make a note of which Goblin Mobs include Nasty Skulkers. These units are referred to as 'concealing' units. At the start of its first round of combat, during Step 1.1 of the Choose Combat & Fight sub-phase, a concealing unit must reveal its Nasty Skulkers — they cannot be revealed at any other time. Position each revealed Nasty Skulker as you would a character that has joined the unit. Once placed, Nasty Skulkers cannot leave their concealing unit. If a concealing unit is destroyed or flees the battlefield before its Nasty Skulkers are revealed, they are removed as casualties.

rules.name: Release the Fanatics
rules.description: Fanatics are concealed within Night Goblin Mobs. The concealing unit may release one or more Fanatics during any Start of Turn sub-phase. A released Fanatic moves 2D6" in a controller-chosen direction during the Compulsory Moves sub-phase of the turn they are released; thereafter they move 2D6" in a random direction each Compulsory Moves sub-phase. Any unit (friend or foe) moved through suffers D6 Strength 5 hits each with AP-3. Fanatics ignore the 1" rule. A Fanatic is removed if it moves into difficult, dangerous or impassable terrain, or rolls a natural double on its movement dice. They are removed as casualties if their concealing unit is destroyed or flees before release.

rules.name: Squigs Go Wild
rules.description: If a Night Goblin Squig Herd ever Breaks and flees, the Cave Squigs go wild. Every unit (friend or foe) within 2D6" suffers D3 Strength 5 hits with AP-1; +1 to D3 per additional 5 Cave Squigs in the unit. The unit is then treated as destroyed.

rules.name: Timmm-berrr!
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook for the Timmm-berrr! special rule (used by Giants and similar large models that may fall over when slain).

rules.name: Giant Attacks
rules.description: Instead of making normal attacks, a Giant rolls on the Giant Attacks table (D6): 1='Eadbutt (D3+1 wounds, no saves); 2=Belly Flop (5" template); 3-4=Mighty Swing (D6+1 attacks, S+1, AP-2); 5=Thump With Club (S+4, AP-4, Multiple Wounds D6); 6=Jump Up & Down (D6+1 Strength hits, no armour save).

rules.name: Bonegrinder Giant Attacks
rules.description: Instead of making normal attacks, a Bonegrinder Giant rolls on its own Giant Attacks table with entries for Little Things (regular/heavy infantry, swarms, light/heavy cavalry, war beasts), Big Things (monstrous infantry/cavalry, light chariot, war machine) and Bigger Things (heavy chariot, monstrous creature, behemoth). Refer to Arcane Journal: Orc & Goblin Tribes for full details.

rules.name: Pick Up And...
rules.description: When fighting models whose troop type is regular infantry or heavy infantry, a Giant may pick up enemy models instead of making normal attacks. Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook for full details.

rules.name: Ker-splat!
rules.description: Mangler Squigs treat all difficult terrain as dangerous terrain.

rules.name: Spiked Ball & Chains
rules.description: Impact Hits caused by this model have an Armour Piercing characteristic of -3.

rules.name: Doom Diver
rules.description: When shooting, after the Scatter roll, the controlling player may roll D3 and move the 3" blast template that many inches in any direction. On indirect fire, if Hit! is rolled, use the arrow above the Hit symbol and move the template Artillery dice minus D3 inches in that direction (minimum 0).

rules.name: Bully
rules.description: An Orc Bully accompanies a war machine and cannot leave it. Friendly war machines within the Orc Bully's Command range may use its Leadership characteristic.

rules.name: Indiscriminate Hunger
rules.description: In combat, a Troll Hag may make a special attack to deal 1 Wound to an enemy unit. She recovers a lost Wound for each Wound caused this way. Refer to Arcane Journal: Orc & Goblin Tribes for full details.

rules.name: Motherly Love
rules.description: Instead of making normal attacks, a Troll Hag rolls on the Motherly Love table (D6). Refer to Arcane Journal: Orc & Goblin Tribes for full details.

rules.name: Slimy Shanks
rules.description: Refer to Arcane Journal: Orc & Goblin Tribes for full details.

rules.name: Da Troll Calla
rules.description: Friendly Troll Mobs within Ogdruz Swampdigga's Command range may use his Leadership characteristic.

rules.name: Protect Da Boss
rules.description: Ogdruz Swampdigga cannot be targeted by shooting or spells if he is within 3" of a friendly Troll Mob, unless he is the closest target.

rules.name: Syphoned Strength
rules.description: Ogdruz Swampdigga applies a +1 modifier to his Casting rolls when he is within 3" of a friendly Troll Mob with Unit Strength 6 or more that is not fleeing.

rules.name: All Sneaky Like
rules.description: 0-1 Goblin Wolf Rider Mob in the same army list may have the Ambushers special rule for free. In addition, Kiknik applies a +1 modifier to rolls for Goblin Wolf Rider Mobs with the Ambushers special rule, and enemy units apply a -1 modifier to such rolls.

rules.name: It & Run
rules.description: If Kiknik Toofsnatcha wins a round of combat, he may choose to Fall Back in Good Order instead of pursuing the enemy.

rules.name: Ogre Charge
rules.description: The Armour Piercing characteristic of this unit's Impact Hits is improved by the unit's current Rank Bonus.

rules.name: Frenzy
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.name: Flaming Attacks
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.

rules.name: Fly (8)
rules.description: Refer to the Warhammer: The Old World rulebook.
